title: Biotech Stages Sharp Snapback — XBI +2.9%, ARKG +4.3%
description: Biotech ETFs ripped higher Wednesday after recent tariff-driven weakness, with XBI up 2.91%, ARKG up 4.30%, IBB up 2.07%, and SBIO up 1.97%. Genomics-focused IDNA jumped 3.12% and healthcare technology HTEC added 2.62% as investors rotated back into innovation pure-plays — a signal the longevity-investing complex is still tightly correlated with broader risk appetite.

title: Fisetin Phase 2 Tests Senolytic in Breast Cancer Survivors
description: A newly indexed Phase 2 randomized placebo-controlled trial (PubMed 41835341) tests oral fisetin in postmenopausal breast cancer survivors who completed neo/adjuvant chemotherapy, evaluating whether senolytic clearance can mitigate physical-function decline. The design is one of the first to put a natural senolytic head-to-head against a clinically meaningful frailty endpoint in a high-risk human population.

title: Healthy For Long: Nine Longevity Startups Cross From Hype to Human Trials
description: Healthy For Long's new field guide profiles nine longevity startups translating seven distinct mechanisms into human studies — covering partial reprogramming, senolytics, NLRP3 inhibition, and stem-cell programs. The roundup signals that the field's narrative is shifting from billion-dollar mega-rounds to actual clinical readouts.

title: Rapamycin Reframed as a Genome Protector, Not Just an mTOR Inhibitor
description: A new mechanistic Aging Cell paper from Oxford and Nottingham (PMC12794675) shows low-dose rapamycin directly reduces DNA damage burden in aged immune cells, cutting p21 and protecting T cells from genotoxic insult. The result reframes rapamycin as a genoprotector with potential applications spanning healthy aging, clinical radiation, and even spaceflight medicine.

title: ARDD 2025 Report: Aging Research Pivots From Observation to Intervention
description: The 12th ARDD meeting report just published in Aging-US frames 2026 as a turning point — partial reprogramming, organ-specific proteomic clocks, and AI-designed proteins are converging into actionable interventions. The report consolidates inflammation, metabolic dysfunction, and senescence as three integrated targets that need combination strategies.

title: Healthy For Long — From Hype to Human Trials
description: Field guide profiling nine longevity startups crossing from animal data to human trials across seven distinct mechanisms — finally putting clinical readouts at the center of the narrative.

title: Harvard Reframes Cancer Genetics Through an Aging Lens
description: HMS researchers reframe how age-driven mutational landscapes shape cancer biology, strengthening the case that cancer and biological aging are overlapping rather than separable problems.

title: Cellular Rejuvenation Has the Potential to Reverse Aging
description: Long-form profile of Altos Labs covers rat kidney rejuvenation, planned pig studies, and Life Biosciences' FDA-cleared human partial-reprogramming trial in optic neuropathies.

title: FightAging — STING Nitrosylation Drives Alzheimer Neuroinflammation
description: Scripps team shows S-nitrosylation of STING at cysteine 148 (SNO-STING) drives chronic neuroinflammation; blocking the modification protects synapses in Alzheimer mouse models, opening a druggable node distinct from amyloid and tau.

title: FightAging — Young-Mouse FMT Reduces Liver Cancer in Aged Mice
description: FMT from young mice suppresses MDM2 and inflammation in old mice; zero of eight FMT-treated animals developed liver tumors versus two of eight controls.

title: Naked Mole-Rat Microbiome Stable for 30+ Years
description: Multi-decade comparison shows naked mole-rats maintain a stable microbiome across decades — unlike mice — reinforcing microbiome resilience as a feature of exceptional-longevity species.

title: Seragon SRN-901 Combo Extends Median Mouse Lifespan 33%
description: Combination of urolithin A, quercetin, NR, ALA and SRN-820 increased median mouse lifespan by 33% and reduced frailty, providing a translational template for multi-target geroprotector stacks.

title: New Senolytic Strategy Targets a Key Vulnerability in Ageing Cells
description: Imperial researchers identified GPX4-targeting chloroacetamides that selectively kill senescent cells via ferroptosis. Combination with cancer therapy cleared persistent senescent tumor cells (Nature Cell Biology).

title: Mesoblast Advances CAR-MSC Platform; 300-Patient Phase 3 Enrolled
description: Mesoblast (ASX:MSB) finished enrolling its 300-patient Phase 3 of rexlemestrocel-L in chronic low back pain and licensed Mayo Clinic CAR technology for IBD and CD19 lupus nephritis programs.

title: INBRAIN Completes First-in-Human Graphene BCI Enrollment
description: INBRAIN Neuroelectronics finished recruiting its graphene cortical interface study, with 8 of 10 patients implanted and no device-related adverse events. Final results expected later in 2026.

title: Sinclair: Human Epigenetic Reprogramming Trial Days Away
description: At Abundance360, David Sinclair said the first human partial-reprogramming trial using three Yamanaka factors will dose a patient's eye to test reversal of vision loss within weeks.

title: Peter Attia Supplements List 2026
description: Updated public list of Dr. Peter Attia's supplements, sleep aids, and prescription medications including exact brands, dosages, and stated rationale.

title: Longevity Biotech Hits $3.74B in Q1 2026 Financing
description: Longevity.Technology's DLT tracker recorded $3.74B raised across 49 financings in Q1 2026, up 56% YoY, concentrated in epigenetic reprogramming and mitochondrial health.

title: Wedbush — Longevity Trade is a $120B Market
description: Wedbush analyst note frames longevity as a $120B addressable market following FDA clearance of the first cellular rejuvenation trial and Longeveron's Phase 2b laromestrocel data.

title: ARDD 2025 Meeting Report — Path to Actionable Aging Interventions
description: 12th Aging Research and Drug Discovery meeting report consolidates partial reprogramming, organ-specific clocks, and AI drug design as 2026's most actionable vectors for human aging interventions.

title: Rapamycin and Exercise: New Trial Finds Performance Decline
description: RAPA-EX-01 in sedentary older adults (6 mg/week sirolimus) found smaller red cells, more platelets, higher alkaline phosphatase, higher HbA1c, and higher LDL versus placebo — a counterweight to rapamycin enthusiasm.

title: Insilico Forms Industry-First Longevity Board
description: HKEX-listed Insilico Medicine (3696) launched the industry's first Longevity Board to oversee aging-research strategy across life models, biomarkers, and target identification.

title: BioAge BGE-102 Phase 1 Hits Best-in-Class hsCRP Reductions
description: Brain-penetrant oral NLRP3 inhibitor showed potential best-in-class hsCRP reductions in obese participants with no serious adverse events; Phase 2 cardiovascular-risk POC due H2 2026.

title: AstraZeneca Tozorakimab Wins Third Phase 3 COPD Trial
description: MIRANDA showed statistically significant reductions in moderate-to-severe COPD exacerbations on top of standard inhaled care, completing a clean three-trial Phase 3 sweep for AZ's IL-33 antibody.

title: Rapamycin Genoprotection in Aged Immune Cells
description: Oxford-Nottingham study (PMC12794675) shows low-dose rapamycin directly reduces DNA damage burden and p21 in aged immune cells, supporting a 3-fold increase in T-cell survival after genotoxin exposure.

title: Fisetin Phase 2 Trial Targets Frailty in Breast Cancer Survivors
description: Phase 2 RCT tests oral fisetin in postmenopausal breast cancer survivors after neo/adjuvant chemo to evaluate whether senolytic clearance mitigates physical-function decline.

title: Multivitamins Modestly Slow Epigenetic Aging in COSMOS RCT
description: 958-participant ancillary study showed two years of multivitamin-multimineral use slowed all five tested epigenetic clocks, with statistically significant slowing in PCGrimAge and PCPhenoAge — equivalent to roughly four months of biological aging.

title: Top Longevity Startups by Fundraising 2026
description: Updated tracker shows Altos Labs ($3.0B) on top, Oura and Retro Biosciences tied at $1.2B; 35 of 94 longevity startups raised in the last 12 months, totaling ~$13.1B deployed.

title: Retro Bio Chases $5B Valuation in Longevity Mega-Round
description: Sam Altman-backed Retro Bio is closing in on a record $5B valuation despite no clinical data yet, deploying epigenetic editing and cell-replacement therapies to add 10 years to lifespan.

title: University of Arizona Launches Largest-Ever Human Rapamycin Trial
description: 720-participant Phase 3 of weekly low-dose rapamycin runs two years dosing plus a third year follow-up, with co-primary endpoints of frailty transition and IL-6 plus epigenetic-clock biomarker panel.
